text_blocks.text: Edward John Noble, the owner of Life Savers
candy, drugstore chain Recall and New York City radio
station WMA, purchased the network for $8 million. Due to
FCC ownership rules, the transaction, which was to include the
purchase of three RCA stations by Noble, would require him
to resell his station with the FCC's approval. The
Commission authorized the transaction on October 12, 1943.
Soon afterward, the Blue Network was purchased by the
new company Noble founded, the American Broadcasting System.
Noble subsequently acquired the rights to the "American
Broadcasting Company" name from George B. Storer in 1944:
its parent company adopted the corporate name American
Broadcasting Companies, Inc. Woods retained his position as
president and CEO of ABC until December 1949, and was
subsequently promoted to vice-chairman of the board before leaving
